name: urgent-mail-alert
label: Watch
situation: I want to send a summary to the owner by email whenever an email with the keyword 'urgent' arrives in the inbox

slots.trigger_action: make a summary and send it to the owner by email (outbound send)

skeleton:
1) Poll the inbox (Gmail) every 5 min, or receive its events.
2) [condition check] Does the subject/body contain the keyword 'urgent'? · If not, take no action and go back to waiting.
3) [pre-action gate / verifier] Even when it looks like a match, do NOT act yet: · (false-positive guard) Re-confirm independently that the 'urgent' condition really matches, once more. · (outbound) Sending the summary to the owner by email is an outbound send, so ★ get human approval first (or show a dry-run preview), then execute. ★ (Never send anything outward before the approval/preview passes.)
4) Make the summary and send it to the owner by email.
5) [record] Write an idempotency key (a dedup marker) for "this one is handled" → so the same trigger arriving again does not act twice.
6) [hardstop] If the daily action count hits the cap (20/day), or the gap since the last action is shorter than the cooldown, hold the action → go back to waiting.

registry.trigger: condition/interval — fires when the 'urgent' keyword is met (or on the 5-min polling interval). (A person runs the /command; the condition/interval triggers inside it — unattended scheduling is a non-goal.)
registry.gate: the pre-action gate — independent re-confirmation of the condition + because the action is outbound, human approval (or a dry-run preview) first
registry.verifier: pre-action gate (false-positive independent re-confirmation) + the outbound action (send/post) executes only after human approval or a dry-run preview passes
registry.hardstop: 20/day cap · cooldown (minimum gap between consecutive triggers) · dedup of repeat triggers (idempotency key)
registry.accepted_signal: a trigger log (when, what fired) + action results (sent / held / skipped) + a tallied false-positive rate
